content.problemStatement:
Vanja and Miksi have already finished everything for this year at their university, so they decided to spend their free time playing a game with a binary sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč (i.e. a sequence containing only integers 000 and 111).

At the beginning of the game, Vanja and Miksi write down the expression ‚ąóA1‚ąóA2‚ąóA3‚ąó‚Ä¶‚ąóAN* A_1 * A_2 * A_3 * \ldots * A_N‚ąóA1‚Äč‚ąóA2‚Äč‚ąóA3‚Äč‚ąó‚Ä¶‚ąóAN‚Äč (note below that each ‚ąó*‚ąó is a placeholder and does not denote multiplication).

The players take turns alternately, starting with Vanja. The game lasts NNN turns. In each turn, the current player must replace the first (leftmost) remaining ‚ąó*‚ąó by the sign +++ or ‚ąí-‚ąí (or, equivalently, by the operation of addition or subtraction).

After the last turn, the boys calculate the resulting value VVV of the expression. If ‚ą£V‚ą£‚Č•K|V| \ge K‚ą£V‚ą£‚Č•K, the winner is Vanja; otherwise, the winner is Miksi.

Please predict the winner of the game if both players play optimally.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ 111 if Vanja (the first player) is the winner or 222 if Miksi (the second player) is the winner.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: K=1K = 1K=1, so if Vanja replaces the first ‚ąó*‚ąó by +++, the value of the final expression will be V=1V = 1V=1; then, K‚Č§‚ą£V‚ą£K \le |V|K‚Č§‚ą£V‚ą£, so the winner is Vanja.
Example case 2: K=5K = 5K=5, but the absolute value of the final expression cannot be greater than 111. The winner is Miksi.
